steps.title: Locate the vertex on the parabola
steps.content: The vertex of a parabola is its turning point — the lowest point if the parabola opens upward, or the highest point if it opens downward. Looking at the graph, the parabola opens upward and reaches its minimum at the lowest point on the curve.

steps.title: Read the coordinates from the graph
steps.content: The lowest point of the parabola occurs where $x = 0$ (on the y-axis, where the parabola is symmetric) and $y = 2$ (reading from the y-axis scale). Therefore, the vertex is at $(0, 2)$. That confirms C is correct.
